findings.claim: The experiment assumes that the held-out test quarter (quarter 16) is representative of future threats.
findings.failure_mechanism: The test quarter may not capture seasonal or periodic patterns in phishing attacks that occur beyond the 16-quarter window.
findings.evidence_test: Compare model performance across multiple non-contiguous test quarters or use a walk-forward validation strategy to assess temporal robustness.

findings.claim: The choice of BPE tokenization may not be optimal for phishing email text.
findings.failure_mechanism: Other tokenization strategies (e.g., WordPiece) might yield different results.
findings.evidence_test: Compare the performance of different tokenization methods on a validation set.

critic_r1_output.summary: The experiment design is generally robust, with a clear hypothesis and well-controlled comparisons. Minor concerns relate to the representativeness of the test quarter and tokenization choices.

adjudicator_output.case_rationale: no advancing findings from critic R1
